verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a testis/sperm-restricted protein lacking a transmembrane domain, predicted to be soluble and largely intra-acrosomal in fresh sperm. After the acrosome reaction, IZUMO4 is exposed in the acrosomal/equatorial region where anti-IZUMO4 antibodies block sperm-oocyte binding (CPA/HZA assays), indicating accessibility from outside the cell. IZUMO4 forms complexes with other IZUMO family members (some TM-anchored) on sperm. Surface display is restricted to the germline lineage and to the acrosome-reacted state — a narrow developmental/cell-state context. Not classical surface receptor (no TM). Maps to tissue_restricted_surface (germline-restricted display via complex partnership with TM IZUMO members exposed post-AR).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether IZUMO4 stays wash-resistantly tethered to TM IZUMO partners post-AR or diffuses away as soluble intra-acrosomal cargo; could alternatively be secreted_only.
